title: Nickdonald’S French Fries

steps.text: Add beef broth and sugar into a large bowl and whisk to combine.

Peel potatoes and, using a fry tower cutter, cut the potatoes into shoestring french fry size and add to beef broth mixture. • Can also cut the fries by hand into 3/8” x 3/8” pieces to replicate the slim profile of a NickDonalds french fry.
Once all fries are added to the beef broth, mix fries around to keep them separated. Make sure all fries are covered with broth and let them marinade for 30 minutes.
Drain liquid out of bowl and dry the fries using a salad spinner and/or paper towels.
Add a layer of fries to a preheated 400°F air fryer, lightly spray the top of the fries with oil, and repeat layering/oil combination until all fries are added.
Cook for 20-25 minutes; tossing every 5-6 minutes. • Every time the fries are shaken or tossed, lightly spray them with oil.
Take fries out, add salt to taste, and shake them to evenly disperse salt.
Split into 5 portions and store them for use as a side dish to other meals throughout the week.
To reheat, simply add them to a preheated 400°F air fryer for 3-4 minutes.

tips: Make sure you buy broth with ingredients that are as close to pure beef broth as possible. You want to avoid ones that include celery, carrot, onion, and/or garlic powder.

title: Mozzarella Sticks

ingredients.item: mozzarella sticks (45 Cal) D R E D G E S TAT I O N 1
all-purpose flour D R E D G E S TAT I O N 2
egg white D R E D G E S TAT I O N 3

steps.text: Set up dredging stations by adding all Dredge Station Ingredients to three separate containers and mixing until well combined.
Add 2 mozzarella sticks to Dredge Station 1, roll around until completely covered, and shake any excess flour off.
Add floured sticks to Dredge Station 2 and make sure they are completely covered in egg.
Lay mozzarella down in Dredge Station 3 and shake the container side to side to completely cover the sticks.
Press breading into the sticks for the most coverage and put back into Dredge Station 2.
Completely soak sticks and move back to Dredge Station 3 for one final breading.
Place breaded mozzarella sticks off to the side and repeat process until all 6 are done.
Add sticks to a preheated 400°F air fryer, make sure they aren’t touching, and lightly spray with olive oil on both the top and bottom.
Cook for 2 minutes, flip sticks, and cook for another 2 minutes.
Plate em’, grab some MARINARA SAUCE , and enjoy.

tips: Keep a close eye on the mozzarella sticks as they will start leaking if cooked for too long. T I P : Put breaded mozzarella sticks in the freezer for one hour or more to allow the mozzarella sticks to achieve a better browning and crisp in the air fryer.

title: Taco Egg Rolls

ingredients.item: Egg Roll Filling Taco Egg Rolls 30g oil (for frying)

steps.text: Add avocado oil to a preheated pan on medium heat and add ground beef to the pan by crumbling in bite-sized chunks.
Add MSG evenly across the beef, break down beef even more using a wooden spatula, and stir constantly until meat is cooked.
Set meat aside on a plate or into a container, wipe down pan, place back on the stovetop, and add avocado oil.
Add chili powder, cumin, coriander, smoked paprika, and black pepper to a small bowl and mix.
Add onions to pan, mix around so oil is spread evenly throughout, and cook 2-3 minutes or until onions become translucent.
Make room in the middle of the pan, add garlic, and stir. Make room in the middle of the pan again, add the seasoning mix, let it sit for 20-30 seconds, then add cooked meat into the pan. Mix well.
Once combined, add beef broth and tomato sauce, mix together, and turn burner to low to let the mixture simmer for about 10 minutes.
Once Egg Roll Filling has reduced, put on a plate, and let it cool for 10-15 minutes.
Into a 5 or 6” pan add oil and turn on medium heat.
Fill a small bowl with water and set aside. Lay an egg roll wrap on the counter.
Into the middle of the egg roll wrap, add cheese and filling, and pack together. Using a brush, dampen a ½-inch square with water on the edge of the wrap.

Bring one of the corners of the egg roll wrap over the filling, tuck the inside of the egg roll wrap into the filling, put water on the corners of the wrap, and fold the corners towards the middle. Brush water on the edges of the wrap and roll it up. • Making sure the egg roll is tightly wrapped is important because if anything gets out, oil will splatter and it not only makes a mess, but it can burn you.
Bring oil in the pan to 375°F. Carefully lay the egg roll corner side down and flip after 2 minutes or when browned.
If the sides of the egg roll need to be fried, use tongs and lay egg roll on its side. Repeat for other side if necessary.

tips: If you want to use the egg roll filling for a different recipe, it works perfectly in the CHEESY GORDITA CRUNCH as well.

title: Mac And Cheese

ingredients.item: egg roll wraps (80 cal per wrap)
egg white Oil spray (4 seconds total)

steps.text: Add noodles and salt to boiling water and cook per label instructions, stirring occasionally so noodles do not stick to the bottom of the pot.
Drain noodles and save some of the pasta water for later.
Add noodles back to pot, put pot back on stovetop, and set burner to low.

Add all Cheese Mix ingredients and stir to combine. • If necessary, add a spoonful or two of pasta water to successfully melt everything together. The goal is to have a thick mac and cheese so that the egg rolls fry up perfectly in the air fryer without leaking.
Separate egg white from the egg yolk into a small bowl and whisk until smooth.
Put 4 egg roll wraps on counter and using a brush, spread egg white on outer edges of the wrap. Add mac and cheese as evenly as possible across the center of each wrap.
Take the corner of the egg roll and wrap it over the mac and cheese. Try and tuck the triangle portion of the wrap into/under the mac and cheese.
Add more egg white to the corners of the wrap and fold in.
Add a little more egg white to the tip of the wrap and finish rolling. Make sure egg roll is fully sealed and repeat.
Place egg rolls on lightly oiled parchment paper and freeze for one hour. • You can cook these right away, but I have found it to be better after freezing.
Add egg white to the entire outside of egg rolls, then add to a preheated 400°F air fryer fold side down and spray the top with oil for one second per egg roll.
After 3 minutes, flip egg rolls and spray each one with oil for an additional second.
Cook for an additional 3-4 minutes, get some low-calorie ranch and ENJOY.

tips: Make double the egg rolls, pull them from the freezer, and air fry whenever you need a quick snack or meal. T I P : Use these as an appetizer or as a side to another low-calorie entrée.

ingredients.item: bag cauliflower rice, frozen B O W L 1 (mixed)
black pepper B O W L 2
avocado oil B O W L 3
green onion, chopped B O W L 4

steps.text: Put dumplings in a bowl with 5g of water, cover 95% of the bowl with a lid or plastic wrap, and cook in microwave according to the instructions on the box, and chop dumplings into bite sized pieces.
Cook cauliflower rice in the microwave for the time recommended on the bag or rice your own cauliflower and cook. While cooking, prep all other ingredients by adding them into separate bowls.
Add avocado oil in preheated high-sided pan on medium heat.
Add green onions, stir to combine, and fry for 30 seconds.
Add the dumplings and stir to combine.
After 60 seconds, crush in garlic and stir to avoid burning.
Once garlic becomes fragrant, add cauliflower rice and Bowl 1, and stir to combine.
Let all flavors come together for 1-2 minutes while constantly stirring. Serve and eat.
Put pre-cooked, frozen shrimp into a strainer, run under cold water for 5-6 minutes or until thawed, pat dry with a paper towel, and chop into small pieces.
Cook cauliflower rice in the microwave for the time recommended on the bag. While cooking, chop the green onion and garlic.
In a separate bowl, mix oyster sauce, soy sauce, MSG, and black pepper.
Add avocado oil in preheated high-sided pan on medium heat.
Add green onions, stir to combine, and fry for 30 seconds.
Add the garlic and stir to combine.
Once garlic becomes fragrant, add peas and carrots and stir constantly for one minute.
Add cauliflower rice and the sauce mix. Stir constantly for one minute.
Add shrimp, mix for an additional 30 seconds, plate it, and eat it.

ingredients.item: avocado oil B O W L 1
slices center cut bacon, chopped B O W L 2
green onion, chopped B O W L 3
garlic, minced B O W L 4 (mixed)

steps.text: Lightly spray a small pan with oil, add an egg, cover the pan, and turn on low.
Cook cauliflower rice in the microwave for the time recommended on the bag. While cooking, prep all other ingredients by adding them into separate bowls.
The egg should be done at this point with a soft yolk and no runny whites. When ready, plate the egg and put to the side. If it’s not ready, give it another minute or two to finish.
Add avocado oil and bacon in preheated high-sided pan on medium heat, stir to combine, and let them cook for two minutes.
Add green onions, stir to combine, and fry for 60 seconds.
Add the garlic and stir for 30 seconds.
Once garlic becomes fragrant, add cauliflower rice, stir to combine, and cook for one minute.
Add Bowl 4 to the pan, stir to combine, and let all those flavors mingle together for 1-2 minutes.
Plate it and eat it.

ingredients.item: garlic, minced Small pinch Mexican oregano Small pinch chili powder Small pinch cumin

steps.text: Add beans to a colander, rinse, and add to a large pot along with the water and salt. Give everything a quick mix.
Place pot on the stovetop and turn up to medium heat. Once boiling, turn the heat to low, and cover with a lid for 2-3 hours or until beans easily smash with a soft press.
Place beans in a large bowl, add the pot back to the stovetop, and turn up to medium heat.
Add bacon fat to the pot. Once melted, add onions and stir until they are coated in fat. • Avocado oil or another high smokepoint oil works here as well!
Once translucent, add garlic, Mexican oregano, chili powder, and cumin. Give everything a mix and add beans back to the pot once the garlic becomes fragrant.
Using a potato masher or immersion blender, mash beans until desired chunkiness.
Simmer beans while constantly stirring until desired thiccness. • Please keep in mind, the beans will be significantly thicker once cooled.
Remove from heat and divide into five portions. • Each portion should be around 170 grams.

title: Foolproof Homemade Marinara

ingredients.item: can Cento® San Marzano whole peeled tomatoes

steps.text: Pour half of the tomatoes into a large bowl.
Add the rest of the ingredients and mix with an immersion blender until desired consistency.
Pour the other half of the tomatoes into the container and mix with an immersion blender again until desired consistency is achieved.
Use right away or store in the fridge/freezer until ready for use.

tips: If you want a chunkier sauce, crush tomatoes by hand and mix all ingredients with a whisk or spatula instead of an immersion blender. T I P : If you want a zero clean up marinara, pour and mix ingredients directly in the container they will be stored in.

ingredients.item: can Cento® San Marzano whole peeled tomatoes

steps.text: Dice onions and mince garlic. Set aside.
Add olive oil and onions to a preheated pot on medium heat and stir to combine.
Add garlic, stir to combine, and let mixture cook for 3-5 minutes.
Add tomato paste and thoroughly combine all the ingredients. Simmer for 1 minute.

Put an immersion blender into the can of tomatoes, blend 15-20 seconds or until smooth, add to the pan with the rest of the Sauce ingredients, and stir to combine. • You can also crush in the tomatoes by hand if you prefer.
Once sauce starts bubbling, give it a stir, and turn burner to low.
Let sauce simmer for 20-30 minutes or until desired thickness is achieved, stirring occasionally.
Serve right away or add to an airtight container and store in the fridge.

title: Spicy Ranch

ingredients.item: salt ¼ tsp dill weed ¼ tsp habanero powder
xanthan gum Taco Bell Spicy Ranch

steps.text: Add all of the Ranch Ingredients into a medium-sized bowl and mix with a spatula.
Into a small bowl, add the Emulsifier Blend and stir until combined.
Add the Emulsifier Blend to the medium sized bowl and, using an immersion blender, blend for 90 seconds or until thoroughly combined.
Serve right away or store in a squeeze bottle and keep in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

tips: If you don’t have an immersion blender you can use a food processor or a blender cup.

title: Chipotle Aioli

steps.text: Add cilantro and chipotle peppers to a food processor and process for 60 seconds.
Add all additional ingredients besides the Emulsifiers and process for another 30 seconds.
Using a rubber spatula, scrape down the sides and bottom of processor.
In a small bowl, add the Emulsifying Ingredients and mix using a spoon.
Once the emulsifier has a smooth consistency, add it to the processor and process for 60 seconds.
Take blade out and mix together manually to make sure everything is incorporated.
Pour into a bowl and serve right away or use a funnel and add to a condiment bottle to use on a plethora of dishes.

tips: If you don’t have a food processor, you can also put these ingredients into a bowl and use an immersion blender to both mix the ingredients and break them down. T I P : If you would like a smoother texture, remove seeds from chipotle peppers before adding to the food processor.

title: Garlic

ingredients.item: black pepper BWW Parmesan Garlic Sauce (30g)

steps.text: Take the head of garlic and chop off the top so that the tips of the cloves are exposed.
Lightly spray top with oil, add a pinch of salt, and a dash of pepper.
Wrap the garlic head in aluminum foil and add to a preheated 400°F oven for 45 minutes.
In a small bowl, add avocado oil and xanthan gum and mix until combined.
In a medium bowl, add all other ingredients and mix.
Add avocado oil mixture to bowl while stirring and combine.
Use a garlic press to add press in 25g of the freshly roasted garlic to the mix.
Give the sauce a good mix, add to a bottle, and refrigerate.

tips: To make sauce even smoother, use an immersion blender for steps 6 and 7.

steps.text: Peel, deseed, grate, and squeeze cucumber with a cheese cloth or paper towels over a sink to try and get as much moisture out as possible.
Add all ingredients in a bowl and mix until combined.
Serve right away or, for best flavor, cover and refrigerate for at least 2-3 hours.

title: Salsa Crema

steps.text: Put all ingredients in a medium sized bowl and mix using an immersion blender for 30-60 seconds.
Optional: Add xanthan gum over the top of the crema and mix in if a thicker consistency is desired.
Serve right away or put into a container and store in the fridge until ready to use.

tips: You can also make this in a food processor if you don’t have an immersion blender.

title: Cilantro Lime Salsa

ingredients.item: Valentina® hot sauce Salt and black pepper, to taste

steps.text: Add all ingredients to a food processor and process for 30 seconds.
Scrape down sides with spatula and process for an additional 30 seconds.
Pour salsa into a sauce bottle and serve right away or refrigerate for a couple hours for peak flavor.

tips: Use within 1-2 weeks or before ingredients are set to expire.

steps.text: Grate onion and add to a medium size bowl.
Put bowl on scale, add the rest of your ingredients, and combine.
Serve right away or let it sit in the fridge for a couple of hours to let the flavors compound.
Add all wet ingredients into a high sided container. • Add 1g butter extract to provide a rich butter flavor to the maple syrup.
In a small bowl, add dry ingredients and mix to combine.
While blending, slowly add your dry ingredients into the container.
Once all dry ingredients have dissolved, continue blending for 2 minutes.
Add the syrup to a pot, put on medium heat, and stir every 2-3 minutes.
Once boiling, allow to boil for one additional minute, remove from heat, and let it cool. • Optional: When cooled, scrape the foam off the top of the syrup and discard.
Add syrup to a container and drizzle on all your breakfast favorites.
Add butter to a pot and put on stovetop on low heat.
Place hot sauce, vinegar, Worcestershire sauce, and corn starch into a medium bowl. Whisk until corn starch dissolves.
Once butter has melted, add garlic and turn up to medium heat. Mix butter and garlic.
When the garlic butter starts bubbling and garlic becomes fragrant, add sauce and stir to combine.
Once the sauce starts to boil, remove from heat and mix to combine.
Add to a container and refrigerate.
Add bread flour, vital wheat gluten, and dough conditioner to a large bowl and whisk.
Pour milk into a measuring cup and microwave for 30-45 seconds or until it reaches 90°F. Add milk to the bowl and using a spoon, mix everything together until the milk has dissolved.
Add vinegar, rinse your hand under water, and use your wet hand to mix the dough until there are no more dry parts.
Once finished, top dough evenly with salt, and cover for 15 minutes.
While waiting, put yeast and water into a glass and mix using a spoon until yeast is covered in water.

Add yeast mixture onto the dough and using a wet hand, squeeze the dough repeatedly for 4-5 minutes or until the yeast mixture and salt is completely mixed into the dough. • I like to squeeze one part of the dough through my fingers, then move to another part and repeat while occasionally flipping the dough.
Cover the dough for 30 minutes.
Using a wet hand, perform a stretch and fold by pulling one side of the dough as high as possible then folding it over itself.
Turn the bowl 90 degrees and repeat 3-4 more times until all dough has been stretched.

Flip the dough over, and form into a ball by sliding your hand underneath the dough and pulling it in a circular motion towards your body. • When the ball is formed, the dough should have a fairly smooth surface and look like kneaded dough.
Cover the dough for 30 minutes, repeat steps 8-10, and cover for 30 more minutes.
Pick the dough up out of the bowl and let it hang to stretch it into a long, narrow piece of dough that is about 5-6” wide.
Lay the dough down with the bottom side facing up towards the ceiling.
Take the top of the dough and fold about a quarter of it onto itself. Using the tips of your fingers, press the edge of the dough into the crease. (RECIPE CONTINUES)
Slowly roll the dough down by continually pressing the seams together with each roll and popping any large air bubbles while doing so.
Seal the roll by pinching along the edge of the dough as well as each side of the roll.
Lightly spray an 8.5x4.5” steel loaf pan with oil, place the dough inside of the pan sealed side down, and let the dough rise in a warm place for 60-90 minutes.
Bake in a preheated 350°F oven for 25-30 minutes.
Take bread out of the pan, add to a wire rack, and let the bread completely cool. This will take 2-3 hours.
Using a serrated knife, slice a couple pieces, and enjoy.

title: Protein Buns

steps.text: Add Tangzhong ingredients into a small pan, whisk until combined, then put on medium low heat. Keep stirring with whisk until it begins to thicken and clump.
Switch to a rubber spatula and keep mixing until there is no more liquid left, or it looks similar to mashed potatoes.
Place Tangzhong in a small bowl to cool. In a separate glass, heat up milk for the dough until it reaches 110°F (about 20-30 seconds in the microwave).
Add Dry Ingredients into the food processor and process for 15 seconds.
Add Tangzhong and butter into the food processor, process on high, then pour in milk over a 15 second period, and continue to process for an additional 30 seconds.
The dough should have formed into a ball. Plop dough on the counter and KNEAD dough for 3-5 minutes.
Lightly spray a large bowl with oil, place dough ball into the center of the bowl, lightly spray the top of the ball with oil, and cover for an hour.
Press any big air pockets out of the dough and divide into 6 equal portions.
Roll each piece of dough into a ball, making sure the bottom of the ball is sealed. Line a baking pan with a nonstick liner, and space dough balls evenly apart.
With the bottom of your palm, flatten each dough ball so that after rising and baking they will look like a bun. Pop any noticeable air pockets with fingers or a toothpick.
Cover buns with another baking pan or damp kitchen towel and let rise for 45 minutes. Place a small cast iron pan on a lower rack in the oven before preheating.
In a small bowl whisk an egg and brush a thin layer of egg wash to the top of each bun. • Optional: Add sesame seeds over the top.
Bake the buns in a preheated 400°F oven, add 4-5 cubes of ice into the cast iron, and shut the oven door right away.
Bake for 17 minutes, flipping the pan halfway through. Place buns on a cooling rack when finished.

tips: If not using within 3-4 days, throw the cooked buns in the freezer and reheat them in a preheated 350°F oven for 10-12 minutes.

title: Potato Buns

steps.text: Add 70 grams of water into a medium size bowl and microwave for 60 seconds.
Using kitchen gloves, grab the bowl out of the microwave, add dried potato, and mix to combine.
Add Dry Ingredients into the food processor and process for 60 seconds. • You may use bread flour instead of 00 flour, but the final product won’t be as soft and pillowy.
Add milk and butter to a glass measuring cup and microwave for 20-30 seconds. Mix until the butter is melted. The temperature should not exceed 120°F.
Add potato and egg into the food processor, process on high, and pour in milk mixture over a 15 second period. Continue to process for an additional 30 seconds.
Place dough on your work surface, KNEAD the dough for 8-10 minutes or until smooth, and round into a taut ball.
Lightly spray a large bowl with oil, place dough ball into the center of the bowl, lightly spray the top of the ball with oil, and cover for 1.5-2 hours.
Divide dough into 6 equal portions, about 67 grams each. Roll each piece of dough into a ball, making sure the bottom of the ball is sealed.
Line a baking pan with a nonstick liner and space dough balls evenly apart.
With the bottom of your palm, flatten each dough ball so that after rising and baking they will look like a bun. Pop any noticeable air pockets with fingers or a toothpick.
Cover buns with another baking pan or damp tea towel and put in a warm place to rise for 60-90 minutes.
In a small bowl, add Egg Wash ingredients, whisk to combine, and brush a thin layer of egg wash on the top and sides of each bun.
Bake the buns in a preheated 375°F oven for 16-18 minutes, turning the pan halfway through. Place buns on a cooling rack when finished.

tips: If not using within 3-4 days, throw the cooked buns in the freezer and reheat them in a preheated 350°F oven for 10-12 minutes.

steps.text: Add bread flour and vital wheat gluten into a large bowl and whisk.
Pour lukewarm water into the bowl and using a spoon, mix everything together until it becomes a shaggy dough.
Rinse your hand under water and use your wet hand to mix the dough until there are no more dry parts.
Once finished, top dough evenly with salt, and cover for 20 minutes.
While waiting, put yeast and water into a glass and mix yeast using a spoon until yeast is covered in water.

Add yeast mixture onto the dough and using a wet hand, squeeze the dough repeatedly for 4-5 minutes or until the yeast mixture and salt is completely mixed into the dough. • I like to squeeze one part of the dough through my fingers, then move to another part and repeat while occasionally flipping the dough.
Cover the dough for 30 minutes.
Using a wet hand, perform a stretch and fold by pulling one side of the dough as high as possible then folding it over itself.
Turn the bowl 90 degrees and repeat 3-4 more times until all dough has been stretched.

Flip the dough over, and form into a ball by sliding your hand underneath the dough and pulling it in a circular motion towards your body. • When the ball is formed, the dough should have a fairly smooth surface and look like kneaded dough.
Cover the dough for 30 minutes, repeat steps 8-10, spray the top of the dough with oil, and cover for 90 minutes.
Lightly dust work surface and top of the dough with flour, degas the dough by punching it down, and flip it onto the work surface.
Using a bread scraper, divide the dough into four roughly 310g pieces and put three of them to the side.
To prevent sticking, spray hands with oil and push the dough outward to get any big air bubbles out. (RECIPE CONTINUES)
Fold the sides of the dough, followed by the top and bottom of the dough, flip the dough over, and round it into a ball making sure the bottom of the dough is sticking together.
Put the dough ball off to the side, cover with a damp tea towel, and repeat with the other 3 pieces of dough.
Let the dough rest for 20 minutes, grab a sheet pan, line it will parchment paper, lightly spray with oil, and put it off to the side.

Lightly dust surface with flour, put one of the dough balls in front of you bottom side up, put a little bit of oil on your hands, and press the dough out into a roughly 8x6” rectangular shape while getting any massive air bubbles out.
Take the top of the dough and fold about a quarter of it onto itself. Using the tips of your fingers, press the edge of the dough into the crease.
Slowly roll the dough down by continually pressing the seams together with each roll and popping any large air bubbles while doing so.
Seal the roll by pinching along the edge, lightly dust with flour, and put the sealed side on the work surface.
Using both hands, start in the middle of the dough and slowly apply pressure both down and out while trying to keep the seam side down the entire time.

Each time the dough is rolled out, apply more pressure, especially at the ends of the dough to give it an authentic baguette taper. • This usually takes about 3-5 rolls and should be about 12-14” in length.
Put the rolled dough on the parchment paper, cover with a sheet pan, and repeat for the remaining dough balls, making sure the pieces of dough are evenly spaced out on the sheet pan.
Let the dough rise in a warm place for 60-90 minutes. • Try and make sure the rolled pieces of dough are as straight as possible before rising or else they will most likely be crooked.

Spray the baguettes with water, score the baguettes with a bread lame or a sharp knife every 2 inches or down the middle. Bake in a preheated 450°F oven with a small cast iron pan preheating on the rack below. Add 5-6 pieces of ice to the hot cast iron pan when you add the baguettes to the oven and close the oven door — this creates steam to give your baguettes a better rise while baking.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, flipping the sheet pan around halfway through and moving the bread on the inside of the pan to the outside and vice versa.
Baguettes are done when you can knock on them and they sound hollow. Let them cool and store any you will not use that day in the freezer.
Add all dry ingredients to a food processor and mix for 60 seconds.
Add Crisco® to the food processer and start blending for 10 seconds. As the processor is blending, add water over a 20 second period and continue mixing for 30 seconds.
Plop the dough on the counter, knead for 6-8 minutes, and divide into 4 equal sized pieces of dough. Each dough should weigh about 129g.
Roll each piece of dough into a ball, making sure the bottom of the ball is sealed. Line a baking pan with a nonstick liner, space dough balls evenly apart, and cover for 90 minutes.
Flip one piece of dough bottom side up and gently roll the dough back and forth with a rolling pin. Continue rolling until about a quarter inch thick.
Pick up the dough and place onto the counter in the shape of a square. Roll out the edges to get an even thickness.
The dough should be about 6” wide and 6.5” long.
Take the top of the dough and fold about half of it onto itself. Using the tips of your fingers, press the edge of the dough into the crease.

Take the bottom of the dough and fold it up. It should perfectly line up to the top corners of the dough. Seal the roll by pinching along the edge and sides of the dough. The roll should be about 6” long.
Dust a nonstick liner with corn meal and place the hoagie roll directly on the corn meal.
Repeat for the other pieces of dough and cover for 90 minutes.
Add a steel brownie pan to the bottom rack of the oven and preheat the oven to 425°F.
Add a piece of parchment paper to a pizza peel or a cookie sheet.

Grab each piece of dough from the bottom, lightly stretch it out from the middle for a couple seconds, and place them on the pizza peel, making sure to evenly space them out. The roll should be about 8” long.
Make a cut down the middle of each roll with a sharp knife or bread lame.
Heat up 56g water in a microwave for 30-40 seconds or until boiling. (RECIPE CONTINUES)
Using oven mitts, add the hoagies to a baking steel, pour the water into the brownie pan, and quickly shut the oven door.
Bake for 12-14 minutes and immediately transfer to a wire rack to cool.
Add all Dry Ingredients into a food processor and process for 30 seconds.
Add avocado oil over the top of the Dry Ingredients.
Heat up water to 110°F.
While processing on high, slowly mix in water over 10-15 seconds and continue to process for an additional 30 seconds.
Plop dough onto the counter, roll it around for 1 minute, form it into a ball, place in a lightly oiled bowl, and cover for 30 minutes.
Divide dough into roughly six 48g portions, roll each piece out using your hands, pop any big air bubbles while doing so, and get them to about 8” in length.
Place each piece of dough on a sheet pan lined with parchment paper or a silicone liner, keeping the breadsticks evenly apart. Cover with another sheet pan or a damp towel for about 20 minutes.
Put breadsticks into a preheated 400°F oven for 12-15 minutes or until bottom of breadsticks are nicely browned.
Add butter, salt, and garlic powder in a small bowl, then microwave until the butter is melted. Mix ingredients to combine.
Brush each bread stick with the butter mixture and top with parmesan and parsley while breadsticks are hot.
Eat one or all 6. Both are great options.

title: Protein Pita

steps.text: Add Dry Ingredients to a bowl and whisk together.
Add olive oil to the Dry Ingredients and mix the oil into the ingredients so it is spread evenly.
Add water to the bowl and using a spoon, incorporate the mixture as much as possible.
Flip the dough out onto the counter and continue incorporating all the ingredients into the dough making sure there are no dry parts.
Squeeze dough ball between fingers, switch hands, and repeat for one minute. This will make sure everything is evenly dispersed throughout.
Split dough into two balls.
Lightly dust counter and rolling pin with flour, put one dough ball down, and add a light dusting of flour to the top of the dough.

Roll dough 2-3 times, turn 90°, roll 2-3 times, and repeat until the dough is about 6” wide and 1/8-1/4” thick. • If dough starts to stick to the rolling pin, lightly dust the surface where the dough is sticking, spread around, and continue rolling.
Lightly spray a cast iron pan on medium-high heat, place one rolled out piece of dough onto the pan, and cook each side for 1-2 minutes or until browning occurs.
Put cooked pita under a damp towel to prevent drying out and repeat for the other pita.

tips: Letting the dough balls relax for 15 minutes after step 6 will make the dough easier to roll out. T I P : Cook 3-4 batches at a time so that you have enough for multiple days. T I P : If you won’t eat all of the pitas within 3-4 days, I would freeze them and reheat them in the microwave or on a skillet.

title: Burrito Tortillas

steps.text: Add Dry Ingredients into a medium-sized bowl and whisk to combine.
In a separate bowl, mix the Wet Ingredients together, add to the Dry Ingredients, and mix with a spoon until most of the ingredients come together.
Dump the contents of the bowl onto the counter and use hands to incorporate until there are no more dry parts left in the dough.
Finish “kneading” this dough by squeezing dough for 30-60 seconds, switching hands with each squeeze.
Divide dough in half, form into balls, cover with a damp towel or wrap each ball individually with plastic wrap, and rest dough for 30 minutes.

Dust counter with flour, plop a dough ball down, dust top of the dough with flour, and roll out using a rolling pin. If dough starts sticking to the rolling pin, add more flour where it is sticking, spread, and continue. • I like to roll the dough 4-5 times then turn it 90 degrees, roll 4-5 times again, and repeat.
Once rolled out to 11-12”, place underneath a damp tea towel or kitchen towel to prevent the tortilla from drying out.
Repeat with the second tortilla and place on a preheated cast iron or griddle on medium-high heat.
Cook each side for 30-60 seconds, flip, and cook another 30-60 seconds.
Put under a separate wet tea towel or kitchen towel until cool.
Use right away or fold and place in a plastic bag for up to 3-4 days.

tips: If storing for later use, put tortilla in the microwave for 10 seconds when ready to eat. T I P : If making a large batch and need to avoid molding, fold twice, place in a gallon bag, and throw into the freezer. When ready to use cover with a damp tea towel or paper towel and reheat for 30 seconds. N O T E : I include an extra 8g into the macros of all-purpose flour that is used to roll the tortilla out, even though it is not included in the ingredient list itself.

title: Nickchicken Meal Prep

steps.text: Add beef broth and sugar to a large bowl and whisk until sugar is dissolved.

Peel potatoes and using a fry tower cutter, cut the potatoes into shoestring french fry size and add to beef broth mixture. • Can also cut the fries by hand into 3/8” x 3/8” pieces to replicate the slim profile of a NickDonald’s french fry.
Once all fries are added to the beef broth, mix fries around to keep them separated. Make sure all fries are covered with broth and set to the side.
Preheat an air fryer to 400°F.
Cut chicken breast into medium-sized chunks, add to blender, and pulse blender for 10-15 seconds or until it is a similar consistency to ground chicken.
Add ground chicken to a bowl, then add salt and erythritol. Mix together until ingredients are thoroughly combined.

Spray your hands with oil to prevent the ground chicken from sticking to your hands, then separate the chicken mix into 140g-sized balls. Form the chicken balls into patties that are slightly wider than the buns and place on a piece of parchment paper.
Set up dredging stations by adding all ingredients to three separate containers and mixing until well combined.
With one hand or your “wet hand,” take a chicken patty, submerge it into the first wet station container, let extra liquid drip off, and place into the Dry Ingredients container.

Using your other hand or what I like to call your “dry hand,” add the wet patty to your dry flour mixture and push flour into the patty to coat it thoroughly, making sure the sides of the patty are covered as well.

tips: putting patty into the dry station. Then you can easily add the dry mixture to the top of the patty after it is already in the dry station container. (RECIPE CONTINUES)

title: $1 Pizza Meal Prep

ingredients.item: part-skim mozzarella cheese, shredded $1 Pizza Meal Prep

steps.text: Place flour, vital wheat gluten, yeast, and salt into a food processor and process for 30 seconds.
Turn processor on high, slowly mix in water over a 30-60 second period, and let dough mixture process for an additional 30-60 seconds.
Plop dough onto the counter, roll it into a ball, and divide the dough into 5 pieces. Each piece should weigh roughly 200-210g.

Form each dough into individual balls by flattening the dough with your fingers, folding each side of the dough into the middle, flipping it over, and rolling it in a circular motion until a ball is formed.

Grab 5 high sided containers, lightly spray oil into each one, put one dough ball into each container, spray the top of the dough ball, cover with a lid, and put any dough that will not be used in the next hour or two in the fridge.
In a separate high-sided container, add the can of tomatoes, garlic powder, and salt. Stir to combine, cover with a lid, and refrigerate.

Spray oil onto your hands and an oven safe pan, plop dough into the middle, start stretching it towards the corners of the pan, and spread it to your desired size and thickness. • If the dough keeps snapping back, cover it for 15 minutes, then come back and finish stretching. This gives the dough time to relax.
Add sauce and spread evenly over the dough with the back of a spoon. Top with mozzarella cheese and bake in a preheated 425°F oven for 12-14 minutes or until cheese is golden brown.

Using a spatula, check the bottom of the pizza to make sure it is golden brown as well. • If it isn’t, you can put it on the stovetop on medium heat and check it every minute or two until it is cooked to your preference.

tips: Dough can be stored in the fridge up to a week and actually develops MORE flavor over time. T I P : You can store any unused dough by spreading it out in a lightly oiled gallon bag, freezing it, and removing it about an hour before they need to be used.

steps.text: Preheat the oven to 500°F, add a sheet pan into the oven, preheat 2 large pans on medium heat, and place a large bowl in the freezer.

Cut steak diagonally into thin strips and transfer them to a bowl. In the same bowl, add soy sauce, salt, black pepper, baking soda, and avocado oil. Massage these ingredients into the steak, ensuring they are well incorporated.
Slice the onions, bell peppers, and anaheim peppers into ¼ inch strips and add to a large bowl.
In a small bowl, combine cumin, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, chili powder, dried oregano, salt, and black pepper. Mix the ingredients together until well blended.
Sprinkle the seasonings over the sliced vegetables followed by lime juice and avocado oil. Using your hands, mix all the ingredients together until they are thoroughly combined and evenly coated.
Using oven mitts, grab the sheet pan from the oven, add the vegetables to the pan, and spread them out evenly using tongs. Place the sheet pan in the oven and set a timer for 10 minutes.
Increase the heat to medium-high on both pans. In one of the pans, add the tomatillos, onion, jalapeño, and serrano pepper. Flip the vegetables every few minutes to ensure they are evenly roasted.

Using the other pan, add 4 grams of avocado oil and half of the steak. Spread the steak evenly over the pan’s surface and brown for 2-3 minutes. Toss the steak, continue cooking for an additional 1-2 minutes, and transfer to a large bowl.

Once the timer goes off, remove the sheet pan from the oven and using tongs, mix the vegetables around the pan. After the vegetables are evenly spread out, place the sheet pan back in the oven and set another timer for 10 minutes.
Finish cooking the remaining steak by repeating Step 8. Add the cooked steak to the bowl with the rest of the finished steak.
Add the roasted stovetop vegetables into a food processor along with ROASTED GARLIC , cilantro, lime juice, water, and salt. Blend the ingredients for 1-2 minutes.
Grab the bowl from the freezer, add the salsa to it, and place back into the freezer for 5-10 minutes.
Once the timer goes off, turn the broiler on high, mix the vegetables around the pan, and broil them for 2-3 minutes.
Add the roasted vegetables to the cooked steak and mix thoroughly.
Into a medium bowl, add tomato sauce, chicken bouillon, garlic powder, onion powder, cumin, and oregano. Mix thoroughly.
Add chicken broth into a measuring cup and microwave for 1-2 minutes or until broth has reached 120°F.
Turn the pan that roasted the vegetables onto medium heat, add avocado oil and rice into the pan, and stir to combine. Frequently stir the mixture until the rice has become golden brown.
Add the tomato sauce into the pan, stir to combine, and cook for 30 seconds.
Add the chicken broth and stir to combine. Once boiling, turn the heat down to low, cover with a lid, and cook for 12 minutes.
Keeping the lid on, move the pan off the heat, let it sit for 15 minutes, and stir to combine.
To assemble, distribute the steak fajita mix and mexican rice evenly among 5 meal prep containers. Fill 2 oz plastic cups with salsa verde, cover each container with a lid, and refrigerate.

When it’s time to eat, place a wet paper towel over the meal prep containers and microwave them in 60-second intervals until they are heated through. For more even cooking, mix the ingredients between each interval.

title: Chicken Noodle Soup

ingredients.item: Better Than Bouillon® roasted chicken base

steps.text: Add olive oil to a large pot and add to stovetop on medium heat.
Chop celery into 1/8 inch slices, add to pot, and stir to coat. Chop parsley.
Remove all meat from the rotisserie chicken and give the celery a stir.
Chop the chicken into bite sized pieces, add garlic to the pot, and stir.
After 45 seconds or the garlic becomes fragrant, add frozen vegetables and stir.
Let everything cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add paprika, black pepper, and Better Than Bouillon® to pot and mix to combine.
Add chicken broth and let it heat up for 2-3 minutes.
Once broth is simmering, add noodles and cook until al dente.
Add chicken and spinach. Gently push down and mix in spinach until completely submerged in chicken broth.
Add turmeric, parsley, and extra chicken broth. Give everything a stir and remove from heat.
Once cooled, add ~830g of soup to 5 containers and refrigerate.
To reheat, add to a bowl, put damp paper towel over the bowl, and microwave for 4-5 minutes.

tips: You can use the left-over chicken bones and skin to make a homemade chicken broth for the next round of chicken soup. I explain exactly how to do this in the recipe video.

ingredients.item: thinly sliced chicken breasts D R E D G E S TAT I O N 1
AP flour D R E D G E S TAT I O N 2
egg white D R E D G E S TAT I O N 3

steps.text: Cut chicken into 140g cutlets.
Set up dredging stations into three separate containers and mix each station until well combined.
Take a piece of chicken and add it to Dredging Station 1.
Cover with flour, making sure to get into all the crevices, and shake off excess flour.
Add chicken to Dredging Station 2 and completely soak with egg, making sure there are no dry spots. Let excess liquid drip off.
Move chicken to Dredging Station 3 and completely cover with the breadcrumb mixture, making sure to press it into the chicken and all its crevices.
Put the breaded chicken on a wire rack and repeat steps 3-6 with another piece of chicken.
Lightly spray the top and bottom of the chicken with oil and add it to a preheated 400°F air fryer.
Air fry for 6-8 minutes or until internal temperature of the chicken reaches 160°F.
Continue breading the remaining chicken and once the first two pieces are finished cooking, put finished chicken on a wire rack and repeat the process until all chicken is cooked.
Toast a bun on the bagel setting to make sure only the inside of the bun is browned.
Add chicken to the bottom bun followed by marinara sauce and grated parmesan.
Add the extra marinara and leftover chicken into separate containers and refrigerate.
To reheat, add the chicken to a preheated 350° airfryer for 3-4 minutes and repeat steps 11 and 12 to plate.

ingredients.item: barbeque sauce (20 cal) Pulled Pork + Mac 160g OG MAC AND CHEESE

steps.text: Drain pork tenderloin, pat dry with paper towels, and add to a large bowl.
Dice onion and red pepper and mince garlic.
Add avocado oil and pork tenderloin to a preheated pan on medium high heat and sear on one side until golden brown.
Add salt, black pepper, smoked paprika, ground mustard, cumin, and brown sugar erythritol to a large bowl and mix. Add the seared pork tenderloin into the spice mix.
Add onion and red pepper to the pan and mix with remaining oil. Sauté for 2-3 minutes or until onions become translucent.
Add garlic to the pan, mix, and sauté for one minute or until garlic becomes fragrant.
Place the vegetables into the slow cooker along with the water, apple cider vinegar, and Worcestershire sauce.
Completely coat the pork tenderloin with the spice mix and place it in the slow cooker with the raw side facing up.
Cook on low for 8-10 hours or on high for 5-6 hours. When a fork easily pierces the tenderloin, it is finished.
Take the tenderloin out of the pot, pull the meat apart using two forks, and give the meat a rough chop. • For a smooth sauce, pour the pot into a high sided container and blend until smooth.
Add the sauce and pulled pork back to the slow cooker and mix.
Make the OG MAC AND CHEESE .
Add pulled pork and mac and cheese to 6 meal prep containers and refrigerate.
To reheat, place a damp paper towel over a container and microwave in 1-minute intervals, stirring between each interval until desired temperate is reached.
Add pork to buns, top with barbecue sauce, and enjoy.
Chop onion, mince garlic, and add salt, smoked paprika, cumin, and Mexican oregano to a small bowl.
Add avocado oil and onion to a preheated pot on medium heat. Stir to combine.
Once onion becomes translucent, add garlic and seasonings to the pot and mix. Transfer to a slow cooker once the garlic becomes fragrant.
Add tomatoes, chipotle peppers, and chicken base to the slow cooker. Stir to combine.

Add chicken to the slow cooker, cover the top of the chicken with some of the tomato mix, cover with a lid, and cook on low for 4-6 hours. • Chicken should be ½ to ¾ inches thick to cook in this timeframe.
Two hours before the chicken is finished cooking, start the REFRIED BEANS .
Finish the beans, take the chicken out of the pot, and pull the meat apart using two forks. • For a smooth sauce, pour the pot into a high sided container and blend until smooth.
Add chicken and sauce back into the pot and stir to combine.
Place chicken and beans in 5 meal prep containers and refrigerate.
To reheat, place a damp paper towel over a container and microwave in 1-minute intervals, stirring between each interval until desired temperate is reached.
Top tostada with ingredients in the order listed and eat.

ingredients.item: butter, melted Oil spray $1 Pizza Pot Pie Meal Prep

steps.text: Add flour, salt, and yeast to a large bowl. Whisk to combine.

Add water and mix the ingredients using a spoon until a shaggy dough forms. Go in with a wet hand, press the dough down, and fold it onto itself. The goal is to get all of the dry ingredients mixed in.
Once the dough is fully hydrated, cover for 20 minutes.
Using a wet hand, perform a stretch and fold by pulling one side of the dough as high as possible then folding it over itself.
Turn the bowl 90 degrees and repeat 3-4 more times until all dough has been stretched.
Flip the dough over and form into a ball by sliding your hand underneath the dough and pulling it in a circular motion towards your body.
Cover the dough for 20 minutes and repeat steps 4-6. Cover for an additional 20 minutes.
Divide the dough into 5 pieces. Each piece should weigh roughly 130g.

Form each piece into individual balls by flattening the dough with your fingers, folding each side of the dough into the middle, flipping it over, and rolling it in a circular motion until a ball is formed.
Grab 5 high-sided containers, lightly spray oil into each one, put a dough ball into each container, cover with a lid, and refrigerate for 24 hours.
Add olive oil and onion to a preheated pot on medium heat and mix. Sauté onion for 2-3 minutes, add garlic and oregano, and stir.
Once garlic becomes fragrant, add tomatoes and salt. Stir to combine. When the sauce starts bubbling or boiling, reduce the heat to low, give the pot a mix, and simmer for 15-20 minutes.
After cooling, transfer the sauce to a high-sided container and refrigerate.
Grab a ceramic bowl that is about 6” wide and 1.5” deep. Lightly spray with oil.
Add slices of mozzarella to the bowl, followed by the fat-free mozzarella and sauce.
Grab the outside of the dough and rotate it in a circle, letting gravity stretch it out until it is big enough to cover the entire bowl.
Lay the dough over the top of the bowl and pat the edges of the dough.
Complete steps 1-3 of the NICKDONALD’S FRENCH FRIES . • You can keep the skins of the potatoes on for this meal prep if you prefer.
Pat the chicken wings dry with a paper towel. Using a knife, remove wingtips and discard.
Using a knife, separate wings into drums and flats. Pat the chicken wings dry with a paper towel. • You can buy flats and drums that are already separated; they will just be more expensive.
Add salt to both sides of the wings and place in a bowl.
Add flour and cornstarch and toss until evenly coated.
Add a layer of wings to a preheated 400°F air fryer, lightly spray the top of the wings with oil, and repeat layering/oil combination until all wings are added.
Cook for 25 minutes, shaking every 8-10 minutes for even browning.
While the wings cook, make the BUFFALO SAUCE .
Complete step 4 of the Nickdonald’s French Fries.
Place finished wings into a large bowl and wipe down the air fryer using paper towels.
Complete steps 5-7 of the Nickdonald’s French Fries.
Once the fries have cooled, separate everything into 5 meal prep containers and refrigerate.
When it’s time to dig in, place the wings in a preheated 400°F air fryer for 6-8 minutes. Add the fries during the last 3 minutes of the cooking process.
Add buffalo sauce and wings into a large bowl and toss to coat. Plate the combo meal and go to town.
